title: Ervatamia evrardii
taxonomy: Ervatamia evrardii is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Apocynaceae, commonly known as the dogbane or oleander family. It belongs to the genus Ervatamia, which comprises about 20 species of tropical plants. This genus is further grouped under the subfamily Asclepiadoideae. Ervatamia evrardii is native to Southeast Asia and is often found in tropical forests and woodland areas. The classification of Ervatamia evrardii is based on its morphological and anatomical characteristics, as well as its genetic makeup.
uses: Ervatamia evrardii has been found to possess various medicinal properties. Research suggests that the plant may be used in the treatment of fever, inflammation, and other health conditions.
